profile.name: research-distillation
profile.intent: Distill exemplary public or sanitized sources into reusable patterns, workflow contracts, and future skill proposals.

routing.handoff_policy: Select the project profile first, enter through discovery-router, and hand off to mature skills before creating new execution logic.

install_policy.notes: Keep this profile local until a separate research-distillation-skills repo exists.

workflow_contract.lane_selection_rule: Use evidence for source synthesis, action for mutation-heavy automation, combined when both are required, and no-contract only for low-risk read-only answers.

required_preflight: Detect scope and git state before mutation.
Name the mature owner skill or wrapper before executing.
Run validation before commit, push, publication, or repo extraction.
forbidden_actions: Do not bypass mature owner skills such as safe-git-ops.
Do not mutate credentials or private paths in public artifacts.
Do not scaffold a new skill before the promotion gate is met.
action_lane.approval_boundary: Escalate before destructive recovery, force push, credential changes, public release, or standalone skill creation.

evidence_lane.confidence_policy: Promote only when multiple artifacts show reusable ownership beyond existing routers and skills.
evidence_lane.citation_policy: Use source cards or repo-local artifact links; do not copy raw private transcripts or long copyrighted text into public kernels.

combined_lane.handoff_artifact: workflow contract or assessment that names both evidence owner and action owner
combined_lane.staleness_trigger: A new profile split, runtime adapter, or repeated live-session lane-selection failure.

no_contract_lane.allowed_when: The request is low-risk, read-only, and creates no durable project state or public claim.
no_contract_lane.escalation_trigger: The answer would mutate files, make a durable decision, publish a claim, or create reusable memory.

acceptance_checks: Kernel JSON parses and the example references real profile entrypoints and skills.
Workflow lanes include action, evidence, combined, and no-contract.
Adapters are metadata targets and not the source of workflow truth.

privacy_boundary.publication_audit_required_when: Use memory-publication-auditor before promoting private-derived examples into public kernels.

selection_contexts: Select research-distillation-workflow-contract directly when the task is to distill public or sanitized sources into reusable workflow patterns, contracts, or future skill proposals.
Use it as a profile-local contract surface rather than as a standalone executor when mature discovery, reading, publication-audit, or skill-system skills own the concrete subtask.
selection_semantics.delegation_policy: Delegate concrete source intake to discovery/reference skills and promotion safety checks to memory-publication-auditor or skill-system-auditor while preserving the workflow-contract artifact as the profile-level schema.
selection_semantics.installable_manifest_gate: Installable manifests should require live evidence that a runtime can select this profile contract for distillation tasks without making it the only source of workflow truth.

promotion_gates: A second repo or profile can consume the same kernel fields without bespoke documentation.
Runtime adapters can be generated from the kernel without changing the source schema.
Repeated live sessions show lane-selection failures that existing routers cannot prevent.
rejection_checks: Do not create a standalone skill if mature routers already own the workflow.
Do not publish private-derived examples without an audit.
Do not make runtime-specific metadata the only copy of the contract.
